D6 Display L1 Remember Atom

Night Shift: Warming the White

after sunset the phone slides its white point toward amber — the same pixels, recoloured by a warmer reference white

Night Shift, Night Light, and f.lux all do one thing: they lower the display's correlated colour temperature. The white point slides down the Planckian locus from a cool ~6500 K toward a warm ~2700 K, cutting the blue content of everything on screen.

Drag the colour temperature from daylight down to candlelight and watch a mock interface warm. Nothing about the content changes — only the white the display renders against, which tints every colour toward amber.
